title: Harden AI provider boundaries and diagnostics
goal: Poword should expose explicit provider and model configuration, use environment-driven secrets where possible, and leave enough diagnostics for operators to understand which AI path executed.
instructions: Review the current Poword AI integration and keep changes grounded in repository truth.
Prefer small, reviewable product changes over broad architectural rewrites.
Do not introduce harness runtime files, status files, or dashboard artifacts into Poword.
acceptanceChecks: Poword can determine provider and model without hardcoded values buried in AI call sites.
Operators can inspect basic diagnostics for AI requests without reading harness internals.
No harness runtime files or status directories are added to Poword.
expectedArtifacts: Reviewable config or docs changes if needed

title: Extract deterministic review word selection from stored study activity
goal: Poword should choose review words from its existing dictionary and study-history data instead of relying on a hardcoded word list.
instructions: Add a small selection service that derives a review list from persisted study signals such as highlighted words, favorites, query history, and review results.
Keep the selection deterministic for the same database state by using explicit ranking and stable tie-break rules.
Update the article-generation flow so it can consume the selector output instead of embedding a sample vocabulary list in product code.
Cover the ranking and empty-state behavior with focused tests.
acceptanceChecks: The product can produce a review-word list from stored study data without editing source code.
The same underlying data produces the same ordered review-word output.
The article-generation flow no longer depends on a hardcoded demo vocabulary list.
Focused tests cover ranking or tie-break behavior and the no-candidate case.
expectedArtifacts: DAO support needed to read selection inputs

title: Add a manual article-generation entry point with topic and review-count inputs
goal: Poword should expose one supported way to run article generation from the command line using configured RSS topics and the selected review words.
instructions: Introduce a small application entry point that accepts a topic or RSS source choice and a requested review-word count.
Route the new entry point through the existing article-generation flow and the new review-word selector instead of requiring code edits in `main` methods.
Use the existing topic configuration keys where possible and print a concise run summary or a clear usage message for invalid inputs.
Document the supported invocation in repository-facing docs.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can start one article-generation run without changing source code.
The entry point accepts a configured topic choice and review-word count.
Invalid arguments return a clear usage or validation message instead of falling back to hidden defaults.
README or equivalent product docs show the supported invocation.

title: Add a configurable daily generation launcher with duplicate-run protection
goal: Poword should be able to run one scheduled daily generation pass from configuration without producing repeated batches for the same day.
instructions: Add a small scheduler or scheduled-launch path that reads its topic, run time, and review-word count from configuration.
Prevent duplicate work by checking whether the day already has generated article output before starting a new run.
Fail fast with a clear operator message when required configuration or runtime prerequisites are missing.
Document the schedule settings and the duplicate-run behavior.
acceptanceChecks: The product exposes a configuration-driven daily generation mode.
A second launch on the same day does not generate a duplicate batch when the day's output already exists.
Missing schedule or runtime prerequisites produce a clear operator-visible failure message.
Documentation explains how the daily launcher decides whether to run or skip.

title: Separate RSS source storage from finalized study article persistence
goal: Poword should preserve fetched source stories and finalized generated study articles as distinct records so one news item can move through the pipeline without title collisions or ambiguous reads.
instructions: Introduce an explicit persistence shape for source articles versus finalized generated articles, either through article fields or dedicated DAO methods, so the pipeline no longer overloads one row shape for both stages.

Update RSS ingestion to save source content through the source-side path and update article generation to save finalized Chinese and English output through the finalized side while preserving inserted-word data.
Make duplicate handling explicit for repeated RSS fetches of the same source article and repeated generation attempts for the same source article.
If the article table contract is not represented in the repo today, add the missing schema or a migration note alongside the existing SQL resources.
Cover the DAO and service lifecycle with focused tests for initial ingest, finalized save, and duplicate-source behavior.
acceptanceChecks: A fetched source article and its finalized generated study article can both exist for the same news item without ambiguous query results.
The generation flow reads source content deterministically and saves finalized output without relying on silent unique-constraint failures.
Repeated ingestion of the same source article does not create duplicate source records.
The repository includes enough schema or migration detail for maintainers to understand the article storage contract.
Focused tests cover source ingest, finalized save, and duplicate-source scenarios.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests for the article lifecycle

title: Restore the generation quality gate and save actual inserted-word results
goal: Poword should only keep finalized study articles after the language and word-insertion checks run through the intended prompts and retry paths, and the saved metadata should reflect what was actually inserted.
instructions: Wire the language and word evaluators to the correct prompt contracts and response fields instead of reusing translation prompts.
Fix the retry flow so language failures route back to translation, word-insertion failures route back to insertion, and successful articles continue forward without duplicate work.
Save the words that were actually inserted into the finalized article record rather than only the originally requested review list.
Keep failure advice visible enough for maintainers to understand why a candidate article was retried or rejected.
Add focused tests with stubbed worker and evaluator outputs for pass, retranslate, and reinsert cases.
acceptanceChecks: Language and word-insertion evaluation use prompt keys that match the expected response fields for those steps.
A failed language check reruns the translation step, and a failed word-insertion check reruns the insertion step.
Successful articles complete the chain once and do not trigger extra retries.
Finalized article persistence records the words actually inserted into the saved article.
Focused tests cover pass, retranslate, and reinsert control-flow branches.

title: Add a generation preflight that blocks unusable runs before AI work starts
goal: Poword should validate the required runtime inputs for article generation up front so a maintainer gets one clear failure report instead of partial work and late exceptions.
instructions: Add a small preflight service for article generation that checks database connectivity, required AI configuration, prompt availability, RSS source reachability, and review-word availability before the pipeline starts.
Return a concise structured result with pass or fail status and human-readable failure reasons instead of letting downstream helpers fail independently.
Run the preflight at the start of the generation flow and stop before any article write when the environment is not ready.
Treat an empty review-word selection as a first-class preflight outcome with a clear message rather than silently continuing.
Cover the preflight outcomes with focused tests and document the supported checks.
acceptanceChecks: The generation flow performs one preflight before AI-backed work begins.
Missing API configuration, unreachable RSS input, unavailable prompts, database access problems, and empty review-word selection each produce a clear operator-visible failure reason.
When preflight fails, Poword does not begin article generation or write partial article output.
Focused tests cover successful preflight and representative failure modes.
Documentation explains what the preflight checks and how to fix each failure class.

title: Keep batch article generation moving and record the latest outcome per source story
goal: Poword should continue processing the rest of a selected news batch when one source story fails, and it should persist the latest success or failure outcome for each source story.
instructions: Wrap per-source generation work so one failing story does not abort the rest of the selected batch.
Persist one latest outcome record tied to the source article, including outcome state, attempt time, and a concise failure summary or retry advice when available.
When a later retry succeeds for the same source article, clear or replace the stale failure outcome and keep the finalized article linkage deterministic.
Add focused tests for mixed success and failure in one batch and for a successful rerun after an earlier failure.
acceptanceChecks: If one selected source story fails during generation, later selected stories still run in the same batch.
The product can read the latest generation outcome for a source story without relying on console output or external log files.
A successful rerun for a previously failed source story replaces the stale failed outcome and preserves the linked finalized article behavior.
Focused tests cover mixed batch outcomes and outcome-state replacement on rerun.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests for success and failure transitions

title: Add a recent generation history and article detail lookup path
goal: Poword should expose one supported way to inspect recent source stories, their latest generation outcome, and any linked finalized study article without opening the database directly.
instructions: Add deterministic history queries that return recent source stories with their latest outcome and finalized-article presence.
Expose a small command-line read path for recent history and single-story detail lookup by source id or title fragment.
Show concise no-result and failed-outcome messaging so maintainers can tell whether a story never ran, failed, or completed.
Cover history ordering and detail lookup behavior with focused tests and document the supported usage.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can list recent generation history without inspecting the SQLite file directly.
History output distinguishes source stories with successful, failed, and not-yet-successful outcomes.
Detail lookup can show the source content and any linked finalized study article for one source story.
Focused tests cover deterministic history ordering and detail lookup behavior.
expectedArtifacts: A command-line read path for article-generation history

title: Add targeted retry for failed or unfinished source stories
goal: Poword should let a maintainer retry only failed or unfinished source stories instead of reprocessing stories that already completed successfully.
instructions: Add a retry path that selects eligible source stories from persisted data by source id, title fragment, or latest outcome state.
Reuse the existing generation flow and review-word selection rules, but skip already successful stories by default unless a force flag is explicitly provided.
Update the latest outcome and finalized article state when a retry runs, and print a concise retry summary with retried, skipped, and recovered counts.
Add focused tests for eligibility filtering, skip behavior, and successful recovery after a failed earlier attempt.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can retry only failed or unfinished source stories without re-running stories that already completed successfully.
A successful retry updates the story's latest outcome and keeps or creates the linked finalized article deterministically.
If no source stories are eligible for retry, the command returns a clear message instead of silently doing nothing.
Focused tests cover retry eligibility, default skipping of successful stories, and recovery after failure.
expectedArtifacts: Eligibility and recovery logic for persisted source stories
Focused retry tests and usage documentation

title: Persist finalized article vocabulary links as first-class records
goal: Poword should keep the actual inserted words for each finalized study article as ordered linked records so later reading and study features do not depend on parsing one text field.
instructions: Add persistence for one row per finalized article and inserted word, preserving the insertion order reported by generation.
Populate the linked-word records when a finalized article is saved and replace stale records deterministically when the same source story is regenerated.
Keep the existing summary text only if it is still needed for compatibility, but make the linked records the authoritative source for downstream reads.
Add focused tests for initial save, rerun replacement, and zero-inserted-word cases.
acceptanceChecks: Finalized article persistence creates one deterministic ordered linked-word record per inserted word.
Saving a newer finalized article for the same source story replaces stale linked-word records instead of accumulating duplicates.
Product code can query the linked vocabulary for a finalized article without splitting a comma-separated string.
Focused tests cover initial save, rerun replacement, and zero-word behavior.
expectedArtifacts: Updated article persistence and schema for linked inserted words

title: Preserve original RSS provenance on source stories
goal: Poword should retain the original source link and feed identity for each persisted source story so generated study articles can be traced back to the news item they came from.
instructions: Extend source-story persistence to keep the original RSS link and one stable feed or topic label alongside the existing deduplication key.
Update RSS ingestion so repeated fetches of the same story refresh provenance fields deterministically without creating duplicate source rows.
Expose the saved provenance on source-article reads so later product flows can show or export it without recomputing from raw feed input.
Document the expanded storage contract and add focused tests for save, rerun update, and legacy-schema compatibility.
acceptanceChecks: A persisted source story can report its original RSS link and feed or topic label from product storage.
Reingesting the same source story updates provenance in place without creating a duplicate source row.
Source-article reads expose provenance directly instead of requiring callers to rebuild it from feed input.
Focused tests cover provenance persistence, update behavior, and compatibility with existing article rows.
expectedArtifacts: Expanded source-story schema and model fields
Updated RSS ingestion and source-read behavior

title: Assemble a learner-ready article packet from persisted study data
goal: Poword should be able to build one structured reading packet for a finalized study article that combines the article text, linked vocabulary, and saved source context in a form that can be consumed outside the database.
instructions: Add a small read service that assembles a finalized article packet from persisted source, finalized, and linked-word records.
Resolve learner-facing vocabulary entries in article order using existing dictionary definitions and translations where available.
Keep saved inserted words visible even when a dictionary match is missing by returning a clear unresolved marker instead of silently dropping them.
Cover packet assembly with focused tests and document the supported packet shape for maintainers.
acceptanceChecks: Given one finalized article or source story, product code can assemble a deterministic packet with article text, saved source context, and ordered vocabulary entries.
Vocabulary entries use existing dictionary data when available and keep unresolved saved words visible with a clear marker.
Packet assembly reads only through product queries and services rather than requiring direct database inspection.
Focused tests cover ordered vocabulary output and missing-dictionary behavior.
expectedArtifacts: A study-article packet model and read service
Dictionary-backed vocabulary assembly for finalized articles

title: Export one persisted study article packet as deterministic JSON
goal: Poword should let a maintainer export one finalized study article packet from persisted data so the article, source context, and vocabulary can be consumed outside the database.
instructions: Add one supported read path that resolves a source story or finalized article from persisted records and writes the assembled packet as JSON to stdout or a requested file.
Reuse stored source, finalized, and linked-word data plus dictionary lookups instead of refetching RSS content or rerunning AI generation.
Keep the exported field shape and vocabulary ordering deterministic so repeated exports of the same data produce the same JSON payload.
Return a clear message when the requested story has no finalized article or cannot be found, and cover export shape and missing-result behavior with focused tests.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can export one persisted study packet by a supported identifier without opening SQLite directly.
The JSON export includes article text, ordered vocabulary entries, and saved source context from product storage.
Repeated exports of unchanged data produce the same field order and vocabulary order.
Focused tests cover successful export, not-found handling, and a story with no finalized article.

title: Render an exported study packet as a Markdown reading sheet
goal: Poword should be able to turn one persisted study packet into a readable Markdown study sheet that can be reviewed without database access.
instructions: Add a renderer that formats one assembled study packet into Markdown with clear sections for source context, article text, and ordered vocabulary.
Keep saved inserted words visible even when dictionary data is missing by showing an explicit unresolved marker instead of dropping the entry.
Route the Markdown output through the same packet read path as the JSON export so the product has one source of truth for packet assembly.
Cover section ordering, unresolved vocabulary handling, and empty optional fields with focused tests and document the supported output.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can render one persisted study packet as Markdown through a supported product path.
The Markdown output preserves article and vocabulary ordering from stored data.
Vocabulary entries with missing dictionary matches remain visible with a clear unresolved marker.
Focused tests cover stable section ordering and representative missing-data cases.

title: Batch-export the latest successful study packets for a selected day
goal: Poword should let a maintainer export a day's successful study packets in one run so recent generated reading material can be reviewed or shared without one-by-one lookups.
instructions: Add a batch export path that selects persisted source stories for a requested day, keeps only stories whose latest outcome is successful and that have linked finalized articles, and exports them in deterministic order.
Reuse the single-packet export and rendering flow instead of duplicating packet assembly logic.
Give each exported file a deterministic name derived from stored article data and surface clear skip reasons for failed, unfinished, or missing-finalized stories.
Cover ordering, skip behavior, and filename-collision handling with focused tests and document the batch export workflow.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can export all successful persisted study packets for one selected day without querying the database directly.
Batch export skips failed, unfinished, or missing-finalized stories and reports why each skipped story was excluded.
Export order and generated filenames are deterministic for the same stored data.
Focused tests cover batch selection, skip reporting, and deterministic filename behavior.
expectedArtifacts: A batch packet export command path
Deterministic file naming and skip reporting

title: Export one persisted study packet vocabulary as a tabular flashcard deck
goal: Poword should let a maintainer export the ordered vocabulary from one persisted study packet as a deterministic TSV deck that can be reviewed or imported into other study tools without opening SQLite.
instructions: Add a small command-line read path that accepts the same single-packet identifiers already supported for packet export and emits one TSV deck to stdout.

Build deck rows from the persisted packet vocabulary in article order instead of re-querying ad hoc word lists, and include a stable header row with learner-facing fields such as word, translation, definition, source title, and article title.
Keep saved inserted words visible even when dictionary data is missing by emitting the row with an explicit unresolved marker instead of dropping it.
Cover argument parsing, row ordering, and unresolved-vocabulary behavior with focused tests and document the supported TSV contract.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can export one persisted study packet's vocabulary deck by source id or source key without changing source code.
The TSV output keeps vocabulary rows in persisted article order and uses a deterministic column order.
Words without dictionary matches still appear in the deck with an explicit unresolved marker instead of being omitted.
Invalid identifiers or arguments return a clear usage or validation message.
Focused tests cover identifier handling, ordering, and unresolved vocabulary output.
expectedArtifacts: A single-packet flashcard deck export command
TSV rendering logic backed by persisted packet data

title: Batch-export daily flashcard decks for successful study packets
goal: Poword should let a maintainer export the latest successful study packets for one day as a deterministic set of flashcard deck files so recent vocabulary work can be reviewed in one pass.
instructions: Add a batch export path that selects source stories for a requested day, keeps only stories whose latest outcome is successful and that have a linked finalized article, and writes one deck file per eligible packet.
Reuse the single-packet deck export flow instead of duplicating packet and vocabulary assembly logic.
Write files in deterministic order with collision-safe names derived from persisted article data, and surface concise skip reasons for failed, unfinished, or missing-finalized stories.
Cover batch ordering, skip behavior, and filename collision handling with focused tests and document the supported workflow.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can export one day's eligible study packets as deck files in a chosen output directory.
Only source stories with successful latest outcomes and linked finalized articles produce deck files by default.
Deck files are written in deterministic order with stable, collision-safe names.
The command surfaces clear skip reasons for failed, unfinished, or missing-finalized stories.
Focused tests cover ordering, eligibility filtering, and collision handling.
expectedArtifacts: A daily flashcard deck batch export command
Deterministic file naming and write behavior

title: Write a deterministic export index for offline study materials
goal: Poword should emit a machine-readable index alongside batch study exports so maintainers can see what was exported, what was skipped, and which file belongs to each persisted source story without opening each file manually.
instructions: When a batch study-material export runs, write one index file in the output directory that lists exported items and skipped items in the same deterministic order as the batch run.
Include stable identifying fields such as source id, source key, title, latest outcome, and the generated relative file path for each exported item.
Record concise skip reasons for ineligible stories and keep the index contract stable enough for later tooling to consume.
Cover index ordering, relative paths, and skip-report contents with focused tests and document the index shape.
acceptanceChecks: A batch study-material export writes one deterministic index file beside the generated output files.
The index includes exported items with stable identifiers and relative output paths.
Skipped stories appear in the index with concise reasons instead of disappearing silently.
Rerunning against the same underlying data produces the same index ordering and item metadata.
Focused tests cover exported-item ordering, relative path generation, and skip reporting.

title: Package one selected day as a deterministic offline study bundle
goal: Poword should let a maintainer export all successful study materials for one selected day into a stable folder tree that can be reviewed or shared without opening the database.
instructions: Add one command that accepts a `YYYY-MM-DD` day and an output directory and writes one bundle directory for that day instead of printing a single packet to stdout.
Reuse the existing per-packet export paths for study content and vocabulary output so the new command assembles files and naming rather than duplicating render logic.
Give each packet a stable folder name derived from persisted source identity and write a top-level manifest that lists packet folders and relative file names in deterministic order.
Return a clear failure when the selected day has no successful study packets or when the output directory cannot be prepared, and document the supported command and layout.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can export one selected day to a deterministic directory layout with no manual database inspection.
The bundle contains one stable packet folder per successful study packet plus a top-level manifest with relative references.
Repeated exports for the same day produce the same folder names and ordering when the underlying data is unchanged.
Focused tests cover empty-day handling and deterministic bundle layout, and product docs describe the command and directory structure.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests and updated usage documentation

title: Refresh an existing study bundle by rewriting only changed packets
goal: Poword should be able to rerun a day bundle export efficiently by updating only the packet folders whose persisted study data changed.
instructions: Add bundle state tracking that records the persisted identity needed to detect whether a packet folder is unchanged, updated, new, or no longer present for the selected day.
When refreshing an existing bundle, keep unchanged packet files intact, rewrite changed packet folders deterministically, and remove stale packet folders that no longer belong in the day export.
Print a concise summary of added, updated, unchanged, and removed packet folders so maintainers can tell what happened without comparing directories by hand.
Cover unchanged, updated, and removed packet scenarios with focused tests and extend the bundle documentation with the refresh behavior.
acceptanceChecks: Refreshing an existing bundle leaves unchanged packet folders untouched while deterministically rewriting only changed packet folders.
Packets removed from the selected day are removed from the bundle and from the top-level manifest.
The refresh command prints added, updated, unchanged, and removed counts for the run.
Focused tests cover unchanged, changed, and removed packet cases, and docs explain how refresh decisions are made.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests and refreshed operator documentation

title: Archive a generated study bundle as a shareable zip export
goal: Poword should let a maintainer turn a generated study bundle into one portable zip file with stable contents and paths for offline sharing.
instructions: Add one archive command or flag that zips an already generated bundle directory for a selected day using only relative paths inside the archive.
Keep archive entry ordering deterministic and derive the archive file name from the selected day so repeated exports are predictable.
Define explicit overwrite behavior for an existing archive target instead of silently replacing files.
Cover archive creation and repeat-run behavior with focused tests and document how maintainers generate and replace an archive.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can create one zip archive from a generated study bundle for a selected day.
The archive contains the bundle manifest and packet files with deterministic relative paths and entry order.
Existing archive targets follow documented overwrite or replace behavior instead of being silently clobbered.
Focused tests cover archive contents and repeat-run behavior, and docs describe the archive command and output naming.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests and archive usage documentation

title: Add a day-level generation report beside the offline study bundle
goal: Poword should export a concise day report that shows which source stories were included, which were skipped, and the latest recorded outcome for each story so maintainers can understand a day's coverage without opening SQLite.
instructions: Extend the existing `export-day-bundle` flow to write one additional day report file beside `manifest.json` inside the bundle directory.
Include every persisted `SOURCE` article for the selected day, not only the successful packets that already get exported.

For each row, include deterministic identifiers and the latest persisted outcome fields such as source id, source key, title, outcome status, outcome time, concise outcome detail, finalized article id when present, and packet folder when the story is included in the bundle.
Keep the report ordering stable for the same database state by using explicit sort rules such as source key and source id.
Do not rerun generation or recompute outcomes during export; rely on already persisted state.
Document the new report and add focused tests for mixed success, failure, and no-finalized-article cases.
acceptanceChecks: Running `export-day-bundle` writes a deterministic day report file inside the exported bundle.
The day report includes successful, failed, and unfinished source stories for the selected date instead of silently showing only exported packets.
Rows for successful stories include the packet folder or finalized article linkage needed to find the exported study materials.
Re-exporting the same day with unchanged data produces the same report ordering and content.
Focused tests cover mixed day outcomes and at least one skipped story that has no finalized article.
expectedArtifacts: A new day-level report artifact in each exported bundle

title: Export a deduplicated day vocabulary deck alongside the study bundle
goal: Poword should produce one consolidated vocabulary deck for the selected day so maintainers can review or import the day's study words without opening each packet folder separately.
instructions: Add one bundle-level vocabulary deck file that is generated during `export-day-bundle` from the successful packets already included in the bundle.
Deduplicate repeated words across packets by normalized word text and use explicit stable tie-break rules so the same data yields the same row order.

For each exported word, include enough detail to study or import it offline, such as word text, dictionary resolution status, dictionary fields when available, and deterministic references back to the source packet or source story.
Preserve unresolved words instead of dropping them, and make their unresolved status explicit in the export.
Keep empty-day behavior clear by still producing a valid bundle-level deck artifact or an intentionally empty file with a documented header.
Add focused tests for duplicate words across packets, unresolved dictionary rows, and stable ordering.
acceptanceChecks: A successful day-bundle export writes one deterministic bundle-level vocabulary deck in addition to the per-packet `words.tsv` files.
Repeated exports of the same data yield the same row order and the same merged word set.
Words that appear in multiple packets are exported once with deterministic packet or source references.
Unresolved words remain visible in the consolidated deck instead of being discarded.
Focused tests cover duplicate-word merging, unresolved rows, and empty-day behavior.
expectedArtifacts: A consolidated day vocabulary deck artifact

title: Validate an exported study bundle against its manifest and packet files
goal: Poword should let a maintainer verify that an exported study bundle is complete and internally consistent before sharing or reusing it.
instructions: Add a small command-line validation entry point that accepts an existing bundle directory path and performs read-only checks.
Validate that `manifest.json` is readable, that its packet count matches the listed packets, and that every referenced packet folder and required file is present.
Check the required packet files already defined by the bundle contract, including source article text, both study article files, and the packet word deck.
Return clear non-zero exit codes and human-readable failure messages for malformed manifests, missing files, and packet count mismatches.
Do not mutate the bundle during validation.
Add focused tests for a valid bundle, a missing packet file, and an invalid or inconsistent manifest, and document the supported command.
acceptanceChecks: Poword exposes one documented command that validates an existing study bundle directory without rewriting it.
A valid bundle passes validation with exit code `0`.
Missing required packet files, malformed manifests, and packet count mismatches each produce a clear failure message and a non-zero exit code.
Focused tests cover at least one passing bundle and representative failure cases.

title: Add a strict completeness mode to day bundle export
goal: Poword should let a maintainer treat skipped or unfinished stories in a selected day as a failed export so the day bundle flow can be used safely in automation without manually inspecting the report.
instructions: Extend `export-day-bundle` with an explicit strict option such as `--require-complete` instead of changing the current default behavior.

Keep writing the bundle directory, `manifest.json`, and `day-report.tsv` when file export succeeds, but return a distinct non-zero exit when the selected day contains any skipped or unfinished source story.
Print a concise operator-visible summary that includes included and skipped counts plus the first blocking reason.
Cover all-success, mixed-outcome, and empty-day behavior with focused CLI and service tests, and document the new exit contract.
acceptanceChecks: Running `export-day-bundle` without the new option preserves the current exit behavior and bundle contents.
Running `export-day-bundle` with the strict option exits `0` for an all-success day and exits with a distinct non-zero code when the day has skipped or unfinished stories.
A strict-mode completeness failure still leaves a readable `manifest.json` and `day-report.tsv` at the requested output path.
Focused tests cover empty-day, mixed-outcome, and all-success cases, and the docs list the new option and exit behavior.
expectedArtifacts: A strict completeness option for day bundle export
Updated CLI exit handling and operator messaging
Focused CLI and export service tests

title: Add a dry-run preview for day bundle export
goal: Poword should let a maintainer preview what a selected day bundle would include or skip before any files are written.
instructions: Add a dry-run mode to `export-day-bundle` that reuses the real selection and ordering rules but does not create the bundle directory or staging files.
Report the planned bundle path, included packet count, skipped story count, and deterministic packet folder names that would be written for the selected day.
Keep the preview output concise and deterministic so maintainers can compare runs or use it in scripts.
Add focused tests that prove dry-run and real export produce the same packet ordering for the same persisted data, and document the preview workflow.
acceptanceChecks: Dry-run mode performs the same day selection and packet ordering as the real export path but does not create output directories or files.
The preview output includes deterministic included and skipped counts plus the planned packet folder names for included stories.
Invalid arguments still return clear usage or validation errors instead of silently falling back to a real export.
Focused tests cover dry-run with no stories, mixed outcomes, and a day that would export multiple packets.
expectedArtifacts: A dry-run preview mode for day bundle export
Focused CLI and export service tests

title: Record file hashes in the exported bundle manifest
goal: Poword should include stable per-file hashes in `manifest.json` so copied or shared study bundles can be checked later without reopening the database.
instructions: Extend the day bundle manifest with an explicit hash algorithm field and deterministic hashes for each exported packet file and top-level report file.
Compute hashes from the written bundle files so the manifest reflects the actual exported contents instead of in-memory strings.
Keep the existing folder layout and packet ordering unchanged while updating the manifest schema in a versioned, documented way.
Add focused tests for hash stability across identical exports and for hash changes when one packet file changes.
acceptanceChecks: The exported `manifest.json` declares the hash algorithm and records deterministic hashes for `day-report.tsv` and each file inside every exported packet folder.
Re-exporting the same persisted data produces the same manifest hashes and packet ordering.
Changing one exported packet file changes only the affected hash entries while leaving unrelated packet hashes stable.
Docs describe the new manifest fields and how maintainers can use them when copying or sharing a bundle.
expectedArtifacts: An updated bundle manifest with file hash metadata
Focused tests for manifest hash stability

title: Add machine-readable day bundle summary counts and decision breakdowns
goal: Poword should publish deterministic included and skipped story totals for a selected-day bundle so maintainers and automation can understand the export result without parsing the full TSV report.
instructions: Extend the exported day bundle metadata to include stable counts for included packets, skipped source stories, and a breakdown by decision reason.
Keep the summary derived from the same selected-day records as `day-report.tsv` so empty, mixed, and all-success days stay internally consistent.
Surface a concise run summary from the command line without changing the existing folder layout or current exit-code meanings.
Document the summary fields and their relationship to the day report.
acceptanceChecks: The exported bundle metadata includes deterministic totals for exported packets and skipped selected-day source stories.
Skipped stories are grouped by a stable decision-reason breakdown that matches the rows in `day-report.tsv`.
Empty-day, mixed-outcome, and all-success exports produce consistent summary fields across reruns.
Focused tests cover the new summary contract and command-line output.

title: Write per-packet metadata beside exported study files
goal: Poword should emit one machine-readable metadata file per exported packet so downstream tools can inspect packet identity and contents without re-parsing the top-level report or TSV deck.
instructions: Add a stable metadata file inside each included packet folder with source identifiers, finalized article identifier, exported file paths, and vocabulary counts.

title: Project cumulative packet completion across ranked unresolved words
goal: Poword should show how many currently incomplete packets would be completed by repairing the first N ranked words together, without double-counting overlapping packet coverage.
instructions: Extend `rank-recheck-words` so each ranked row includes cumulative unique packet-completion impact through that row in the published ranking order.
Compute cumulative values from the same unresolved packet references already used for per-word impact and keep the existing deterministic row ordering.
Add a top-level total for the maximum incomplete-packet completion reachable if every remaining ranked word were repaired.
Cover overlapping-packet scenarios so cumulative counts do not overstate the repair lift when multiple words block the same packet.
acceptanceChecks: Each ranking row reports both single-word impact and cumulative impact without exceeding the selected bundles' incomplete packet count.
Overlapping packet coverage across multiple words is counted once in cumulative totals.
Repeated runs against unchanged bundles and dictionary state produce byte-identical ranking JSON.
Docs explain the difference between per-word impact and cumulative impact.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests for overlapping and non-overlapping cumulative impact cases

title: Save unresolved-word ranking results as a deterministic report file
goal: Poword should let maintainers persist one ranked unresolved-word report to disk so the exact bundle set and dictionary snapshot can be reviewed or shared without scraping stdout.
instructions: Add an optional output-path argument to `rank-recheck-words` that writes the machine-readable ranking result to disk atomically while preserving the existing stdout response.
Include deterministic fingerprints for the consulted bundle set and consulted dictionary snapshot in the saved report so later comparisons can verify what was ranked.
Fail clearly for blank, unwritable, or directory-valued output targets and do not leave partial files behind.
Document the saved report contract, rerun behavior, and read-only guarantee.
acceptanceChecks: The ranking command can write one deterministic report file without mutating bundle files or dictionary rows.
Repeated runs with the same inputs rewrite byte-identical report content.
The saved report includes enough fingerprint data to identify the consulted bundle set and dictionary snapshot.
Invalid output targets fail before any partial report file is published.
expectedArtifacts: A saved ranking report file contract
CLI support for writing ranking output to disk
Focused tests for atomic write and invalid-path behavior

title: Compare two saved unresolved-word ranking reports
goal: Poword should show which unresolved words improved, regressed, appeared, or disappeared between two saved ranking reports after dictionary changes.
instructions: Add a read-only comparison command that loads two saved ranking reports, validates their contract version, and diffs rows by word.
Report before-and-after values for rank position, projected packet-completion impact, bundle count, packet count, and unresolved occurrence count, plus a movement classification for each word.
Include deterministic aggregate counts for added, removed, improved, regressed, and unchanged words.
Fail with a clear error when a report file is invalid or does not match the expected saved-ranking contract.
acceptanceChecks: Two identical saved ranking reports produce a deterministic no-change diff.
Words that appear, disappear, or move in impact or rank are classified correctly with before-and-after metrics.
Invalid or incompatible report files fail with a clear operator-visible error.
Docs explain how to use the comparison after dictionary repairs or bundle rechecks.
expectedArtifacts: Focused tests for unchanged, improved, regressed, added, and removed words

title: Generate a prioritized repair template from top-ranked unresolved words across selected bundles
goal: Poword should let a maintainer turn the highest-impact unresolved words from selected published bundles into one fillable repair template without manually copying words out of ranking output.
instructions: Add a read-only command that accepts repeated bundle directories plus an explicit top-word count and generates one combined repair template for the first N words from the current cross-bundle ranking order.
Reuse the existing bundle validation, recheck, and ranking logic so already repaired words stay excluded and selected words remain ordered by the current ranking contract.

Write deterministic template, worksheet, and metadata artifacts for the selected ranked subset, including the chosen word count, selected word list, consulted bundle set, and projected packet-completion totals for that subset.
Fail before publishing partial artifacts when the bundle inputs are invalid, the requested word count is not positive, or the ranked selection is empty.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can generate one fillable repair template for the first N ranked unresolved words across selected bundles without editing files by hand.
The selected words are deduplicated and remain in the same deterministic order as the current ranking output.
The generated artifacts are byte-identical across repeated runs when the bundle set, dictionary state, and requested word count are unchanged.
Invalid bundle inputs or invalid word-count arguments fail with a clear operator-facing error before partial output is written.
expectedArtifacts: Deterministic ranked template, worksheet, and metadata artifacts

title: Report per-bundle completion frontiers for a prioritized repair set
goal: Poword should show how far down a prioritized repair set a maintainer must go before each selected bundle becomes vocabulary-complete.
instructions: Extend the prioritized repair output to include one deterministic per-bundle frontier summary derived from the current ranked word order and unresolved packet coverage.
For each selected bundle, report the smallest ranked prefix that would fully repair that bundle, along with the frontier word, required word count, and resulting packet-completeness totals.
Handle already-complete bundles explicitly so they do not appear as needing additional ranked words.
Add focused tests for overlapping packet coverage, bundles that complete at different ranked prefixes, and deterministic ordering by selected date then bundle path.
acceptanceChecks: Prioritized repair output includes one per-bundle frontier summary for every selected bundle.
Each frontier summary identifies the minimal ranked prefix needed to make that bundle complete under the current unresolved state.
Already-complete bundles are clearly distinguished from bundles that still require repairs.
The reported frontier math stays consistent with the current cumulative packet-completion behavior.
expectedArtifacts: Per-bundle completion-frontier data in prioritized repair output

title: Replay a prioritized repair template from saved selection metadata
goal: Poword should let a maintainer recreate the same prioritized repair template later without re-entering the selected bundle list and ranking slice by hand.
instructions: Record enough machine-readable selection metadata beside each prioritized repair template to capture the consulted bundle set, bundle fingerprints, ranking order, and selected word-count rule.
Add a replay command that reloads that metadata, revalidates the recorded bundle fingerprints and ranked word order, and regenerates the template artifacts when the inputs still match.
Fail fast with a clear operator-facing error when the recorded bundle set, fingerprints, or selected ranked words no longer match the current repository state.
Add focused tests for deterministic replay on unchanged inputs and stale-plan rejection when bundle contents or ranking inputs drift.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can replay a previously generated prioritized repair plan without manually retyping bundle paths or word-selection settings.
Replayed artifacts are byte-identical to the original outputs when the saved inputs are unchanged.
Bundle or ranking drift is detected before regeneration, with a clear failure message instead of silent mismatch.
Replay behavior is covered by focused CLI and service tests.

title: Import a filled prioritized repair set into the local dictionary
goal: Poword should let a maintainer turn one ranked multi-bundle repair set into actual dictionary inserts and updates with pre-write validation and one post-write impact summary.
instructions: Add one maintainer-facing import path that reads `prioritized-dictionary-repair-template.tsv` and `prioritized-dictionary-repair-metadata.json` from a prioritized repair output directory.
Validate the filled template against the saved ranked word selection and selected bundle metadata, and fail before writing if the prioritized inputs are stale, inconsistent, or ambiguous.
Reuse the same normalization and diff rules already used by repair preview, then apply only the required `dict` inserts and updates in one transaction.
After commit, emit deterministic JSON with action counts, affected words, and before-versus-after coverage totals across the saved bundle set, and document the supported invocation.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can import one filled prioritized repair directory without manual SQL edits.
Invalid, stale, or inconsistent prioritized inputs fail before any dictionary row is inserted or updated.
A successful import updates only the selected words and reports deterministic before-and-after coverage totals across the saved bundle set.
Focused tests cover insert, update, no-op, and pre-write validation failure paths.
expectedArtifacts: Dictionary write-path support for prioritized repairs

title: Persist a deterministic receipt for prioritized repair imports
goal: Each successful prioritized repair pass should leave a machine-readable receipt that records exactly what changed and what bundle coverage improved.
instructions: Write a fixed-name JSON receipt beside the prioritized repair files after a successful prioritized import.
Record stable fingerprints for the imported template and metadata plus before-and-after dictionary fingerprints for the selected word set.
Include one deterministic row per selected word with action, dictionary row id, normalized before and after values, and the saved bundle and packet impact context for that word.
Add bundle-level before-and-after coverage totals to the receipt and document the receipt contract for maintainers.
acceptanceChecks: A successful prioritized import creates one deterministic receipt file in the prioritized repair directory.
The receipt is sufficient to audit every selected word as INSERT, UPDATE, or NO_OP with stable before and after values.
Receipt bundle totals line up with the import summary and the saved prioritized repair metadata.
Focused tests cover mixed import actions and deterministic receipt content.
expectedArtifacts: A prioritized repair import receipt file

title: Roll back a prioritized repair import from its saved receipt
goal: Poword should let a maintainer safely undo one prioritized repair pass when a filled repair set turns out to be wrong.
instructions: Add one rollback path that reads the saved prioritized import receipt and restores previous dictionary values for updated rows while deleting rows that were inserted by that import.
Refuse rollback when current dictionary rows no longer match the imported state recorded in the receipt, and report row-level conflicts before any write begins.
After rollback, emit deterministic JSON with restored action counts and before-versus-after coverage totals across the same saved bundle set.
Document the rollback invocation, receipt requirements, and conflict behavior.
acceptanceChecks: A successful rollback restores updated rows and removes inserted rows using only the saved receipt data.
If any selected row has drifted since import, rollback fails without partial dictionary changes and reports which rows blocked the rollback.
The rollback summary reports deterministic action counts and bundle coverage deltas for the same bundle set.
Focused tests cover full rollback, inserted-row removal, updated-row restore, and conflict refusal.

title: Audit a saved prioritized repair receipt against the current dictionary and bundle state
goal: Poword should let a maintainer confirm whether a past prioritized repair import still matches the current dictionary rows and the published bundles it targeted.
instructions: Add one command that reads a saved prioritized repair receipt and validates its required fields before any comparison work starts.
Compare the receipt's saved bundle fingerprints and imported after-values against the current published bundle roots and the current dict rows for the same words.
Return deterministic JSON with receipt identity, bundle-match status, stable per-word audit rows, and summary counts for unchanged, drifted, missing, or mismatched state.
Fail clearly when the receipt file is missing, malformed, or points at bundle roots that no longer exist.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can point Poword at one saved prioritized repair receipt and get a deterministic audit result without editing source code.
The audit reports whether each referenced bundle still matches the saved fingerprint and whether each imported word still matches the saved after-import dictionary values.
Rows and summary counts stay stable across repeated runs against unchanged inputs.
Malformed receipts or missing referenced bundles fail with a clear operator-visible error.
expectedArtifacts: A prioritized repair receipt audit command

title: Generate the next prioritized repair set directly from a saved prioritized repair receipt
goal: Poword should let a maintainer continue the next repair pass for the same published bundle set without re-entering bundle directories.
instructions: Add one command that reads a saved prioritized repair receipt and reuses its saved bundle set as the authoritative input selection.

Re-run the current remaining-unresolved ranking against those bundles and the current dictionary, then write a new prioritized repair template, worksheet, and metadata set to a requested output directory.
Keep ordering, selection, and metadata rules aligned with the existing prioritized repair generation flow so repeated runs remain deterministic.
Surface a clear zero-selection result when the receipt's bundle set is now fully resolved instead of writing confusing partial artifacts.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can generate a new prioritized repair set from a saved receipt plus an output directory, without retyping bundle paths.
Words already resolved by the current dictionary are not selected again in the new prioritized repair set.
When the saved bundle set is fully resolved, Poword returns a deterministic zero-selection result with no misleading partial files.
Documentation explains how the receipt-based follow-up flow relates to the existing prioritized repair commands.
expectedArtifacts: A receipt-based follow-up prioritized repair command
Deterministic prioritized repair artifacts for the next pass

title: Compare two prioritized repair receipts and summarize coverage progress
goal: Poword should help maintainers understand what changed between two repair passes without hand-diffing receipt JSON.
instructions: Add one command that loads two saved prioritized repair receipts, validates them, and compares their selected bundle sets, imported words, and saved coverage totals.
Report deterministic summary deltas plus stable per-word classifications such as retained, newly repaired, reverted, or scope-changed.
Make bundle-scope differences explicit so maintainers can tell whether a coverage change came from dictionary edits or a different selected bundle set.
Fail clearly when either receipt is malformed or missing required referenced files.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can compare two saved prioritized repair receipts and get deterministic JSON with scope and coverage deltas.
When the two receipts target the same bundle set, the report distinguishes newly added, removed, and changed repaired words.
When bundle scopes differ, the output surfaces the scope mismatch explicitly instead of silently combining totals.
Focused tests cover same-scope comparison and scope-changed comparison.
expectedArtifacts: A prioritized repair receipt comparison command

title: Make prioritized repair receipt audits automation-friendly
goal: Poword should let a maintainer audit one saved prioritized repair receipt in unattended workflows by optionally saving the audit result to disk and failing fast when the receipt has drifted.
instructions: Extend `audit-prioritized-repair-receipt` with an explicit report output option so maintainers can persist the exact audit payload as deterministic JSON without relying on shell redirection.
Add a strict mode that returns a dedicated non-zero exit when any saved bundle fingerprint or saved dictionary `afterValues` no longer match the current state.
Keep the command read-only and preserve the current deterministic JSON contract for bundle rows and word rows.
Cover matching, drifted, and invalid output target behavior with focused service and CLI tests.
Document the saved-report and strict-mode workflow in the operator docs.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can run one receipt audit with an explicit output path and get a deterministic JSON report file.
Repeated strict audits over unchanged receipt, bundle, and dictionary inputs produce byte-identical report content and exit successfully.
If any bundle fingerprint or dictionary row has drifted, strict mode exits non-zero while still surfacing the deterministic audit payload.
Focused tests cover match, drift, and invalid-output-path cases, and the docs show the supported invocation.
expectedArtifacts: Updated prioritized receipt audit CLI behavior

title: Compare two saved prioritized repair audit reports
goal: Poword should help a maintainer see how receipt drift changed between two audit runs without manually diffing large JSON files.
instructions: Add a read-only comparison command for two saved prioritized repair audit reports.
Require both reports to describe the same receipt lineage by checking stable identifiers such as receipt path, template fingerprint, and ranked word set before producing a comparison.
Report deterministic summary deltas for bundle fingerprint matches, dictionary matches, missing words, and per-word status transitions such as newly drifted or newly resolved rows.
Sort output deterministically and keep the comparison focused on actionable differences rather than replaying the full underlying reports.
Add focused tests for identical reports, newly introduced drift, resolved drift, and incompatible report lineage.
acceptanceChecks: Comparing the same saved audit report against itself yields zero status deltas and deterministic output.
If a later audit introduces new bundle or dictionary drift, the comparison output identifies the newly drifted rows and updated summary counts.
If a later audit resolves prior drift, the comparison output identifies the resolved rows and updated summary counts.
Reports from different receipt lineage fail with a clear operator-facing error instead of producing a misleading diff.

title: Audit several saved prioritized repair receipts in one run
goal: Poword should let a maintainer refresh the current status of multiple saved prioritized repair receipts together and see which repair sets still match the current dictionary and published bundles.
instructions: Add a batch audit command that accepts repeated saved receipt inputs and reuses the existing single-receipt audit logic per receipt.
Return one deterministic rollup with per-receipt summary rows and aggregate counts for fully matching, bundle-drifted, dictionary-drifted, and invalid receipts.
Keep receipt ordering stable and make duplicate receipt arguments fail before any audit work starts.
Ensure one drifted receipt does not hide the results for later valid receipts in the same batch.
Add focused tests for all-match, mixed drift, duplicate input, and invalid-receipt scenarios, and document the batch workflow.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can audit several saved prioritized repair receipts in one command without manually looping in the shell.
The batch output includes deterministic per-receipt rows plus aggregate counts for matching and drifted receipts.
A drifted receipt is reported in-place and does not prevent later valid receipts from being audited in the same run.
Duplicate receipt arguments and malformed receipt files fail with clear operator-facing diagnostics, and docs show the supported invocation.
expectedArtifacts: A batch prioritized receipt audit command
